name: Endocrine Signaling Process
description: Endocrine Signaling Process in Animals. This process represents a higher-level biological mechanism distinct from molecular/biochemical processes covered in GLMP.
mermaid:
graph TD
    A47[Stimulus Detection] --> B47[Hormone Synthesis]
    B47 --> C47[Gland Secretion]
    C47 --> D47[Blood Transport]
    D47 --> E47[Target Tissue]
    E47 --> F47[Receptor Binding]
    F47 --> G47[Signal Transduction]
    G47 --> H47[Cellular Response]
    
    style A47 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style B47 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000
    style C47 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style D47 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style E47 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000
    style F47 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style G47 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style H47 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Programming Framework: A Universal Process Visualization Methodology
sources.notes: Higher-level biological process visualization: Endocrine Signaling Process. Created using the Programming Framework methodology for organismal and ecological process analysis.

sources.title: Current concepts of hormone action: a historical perspective
G proteins: transducers of receptor-generated signals
The nuclear receptor superfamily: the first 25 years

notes: Higher-level biological process. GLMP covers biochemical/molecular processes; this database covers organismal/ecological processes.
